name: Babesiosis (Tick Fever / Redwater)
description: Tick-borne protozoal infection causing haemolytic anaemia and haemoglobinuria (red urine / redwater). Caused by Babesia bovis or Babesia bigemina.
vision_markers: red or dark urine visible near bedding

diagnostic_criteria.definitive: Blood smear – Babesia parasites in erythrocytes

treatment_info.veterinary_treatment: Specific anti-protozoal drugs (Diminazene aceturate or Imidocarb dipropionate) – vet prescription only
supportive_care: Blood transfusion in severe anaemia (vet decision)
treatment_info.first_aid: Isolate animal. Remove ticks. Keep in cool shade. Contact vet urgently.
treatment_info.do_not: Do not stress the animal – movement worsens haemolysis
